message:
beads: fcp-host policy eval audit — d4cij [HIGH] + zfogg [LOW closed]

Closing the fcp-host policy-evaluation audit.

  d4cij [HIGH]   verify_live_request calls simulate_policy_decision
                 with host_runtime_policy(zone), a fresh in-process
                 ZonePolicyObject whose principal/connector/capability
                 allow+deny lists, capability_ceiling, posture, and
                 usage_budget are ALL empty/None. check_pattern_lists
                 short-circuits on is_empty for every rule type, so
                 rule-based denial cannot fire — only taint flags,
                 approval-token mismatches, and freshness checks can
                 reject. No zone-policy store exists anywhere in the
                 fcp-host tree (grep confirms), so there's not even
                 a config pipeline to feed the gate.

                 The PreflightFailed("policy denied live request")
                 error path in bin/fcp-host.rs:1416 advertises
                 enforcement that structurally cannot happen for
                 rule-based denials. Classic default-allow regression:
                 the shape of the gate is right, the config pipeline
                 is missing.

  zfogg [LOW]    Closed as null-finding. Rule-order determinism is
                 clean (fixed deny-then-allow sequence, .any() short-
                 circuit, no HashMap iteration in denial paths). No
                 policy cache exists (host_runtime_policy rebuilds
                 fresh per request; BudgetPolicyEngine reads live
                 under RwLock). DiscoveryCache affects visibility,
                 not decisions. Filed for audit trail so future
                 reviewers don't re-audit these angles.

No patches shipped — the HIGH remediation is a new feature (zone-
policy store + admin endpoint + AppState plumbing), not a review-
scope fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:12:38.498519145Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:15.165393Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:15.165379Z","close_reason":"Verified via rch exec with isolated target dir: cargo test -p fcp-e2e --test datadog_compliance_e2e --features datadog -- --nocapture (3/3 passing).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","e2e","fcp2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-e3i9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":997,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: E2E compliance tests written and passing (default_deny + happy_path + network_guard). Blocked by provisioning bead.","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:22:00Z"},{"id":1026,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"E2E compliance tests fixed and passing (3/3): default_deny, allow_valid_token, manifest_network_guard.","created_at":"2026-03-07T21:10:23Z"}]} {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d1b","title":"fcp.microsoft365: Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector","description":"# FCP Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector ## Overview Comprehensive enterprise connector for Microsoft 365 suite, providing unified access through Microsoft Graph API. Critical for enterprise adoption with support for Outlook, OneDrive, Excel, Word, OneNote, SharePoint, and MS Planner. ## Sub-Connectors Architecture ### 1. Outlook (Email & Calendar) #### Email Operations - **Read Messages**: Inbox, folders, search - **Send Messages**: Compose, reply, forward - **Manage Messages**: Move, delete, archive - **Attachments**: Upload, download, inline - **Mail Folders**: Create, organize, rules #### Calendar Operations - **Events**: Create, update, delete, RSVP - **Calendars**: Multiple calendar support - **Availability**: Free/busy lookup - **Scheduling**: Find meeting times - **Recurring Events**: Complex patterns #### Contacts - **Contact Management**: CRUD operations - **Contact Folders**: Organization - **Contact Groups**: Distribution lists ### 2. OneDrive (File Storage) #### File Operations - **Upload**: Simple and resumable - **Download**: Direct and streaming - **Copy/Move**: Within and across drives - **Delete**: Soft delete and restore - **Sharing**: Links, permissions #### Folder Management - **Create/Delete Folders** - **List Contents**: Pagination support - **Search**: Full-text search - **Delta Sync**: Track changes #### Special Folders - Documents, Pictures, Desktop - App-specific folders - Shared folders ### 3. Excel Online #### Workbook Operations - **Open/Create Workbooks** - **List Worksheets** - **Workbook Sessions**: Persistent editing #### Data Operations ```python # Read range GET /workbook/worksheets/{id}/range(address='A1:D10') # Update range PATCH /workbook/worksheets/{id}/range(address='A1:D10') { "values": [["A", "B"], ["C", "D"]] } ``` #### Table Operations - **Create Tables**: From ranges - **Add Rows**: Append data - **Sort/Filter**: Data manipulation - **Calculated Columns**: Formulas #### Charts & Pivots - Create charts from data - Pivot table operations ### 4. Word Online #### Document Operations - **Create Documents**: From templates - **Read Content**: Full text extraction - **Update Content**: Append, replace - **Export**: PDF, other formats #### Content Controls - Find and replace - Bookmark management - Comment operations ### 5. OneNote #### Notebook Operations - **List Notebooks**: Personal and shared - **Create Notebooks** - **Sections**: Create, organize - **Section Groups**: Nested organization #### Page Operations ```python # Create page with HTML content POST /sections/{id}/pages Content-Type: text/html Page Title

Heading

Content here

``` #### Content - Rich text with HTML - Images and attachments - Ink and handwriting - Tags and to-do items ### 6. SharePoint #### Site Operations - **List Sites**: All accessible sites - **Site Details**: Properties, permissions - **Subsites**: Create, manage #### Lists & Libraries - **Document Libraries**: File storage - **Custom Lists**: Structured data - **List Items**: CRUD operations - **Views**: Custom views #### Pages & News - **Site Pages**: Create, update - **News Posts**: Publishing - **Web Parts**: Content blocks ### 7. MS Planner #### Plan Operations - **Create Plans**: Within Groups - **List Plans**: User's plans - **Plan Details**: Buckets, tasks #### Task Operations ```python { "planId": "...", "bucketId": "...", "title": "Task title", "assignments": { "user-id": { "@odata.type": "#microsoft.graph.plannerAssignment", "orderHint": " !" } }, "dueDateTime": "2024-12-31T00:00:00Z" } ``` #### Bucket Management - Create/organize buckets - Move tasks between buckets - Bucket ordering ## Microsoft Graph API (Unified Endpoint) ### Base Configuration ```yaml graph_api: base_url: "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0" beta_url: "https://graph.microsoft.com/beta" scopes: - "User.Read" - "Mail.ReadWrite" - "Files.ReadWrite.All" - "Calendars.ReadWrite" - "Sites.ReadWrite.All" - "Tasks.ReadWrite" ``` ### Batch Requests ```python POST /$batch { "requests": [ {"id": "1", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/messages"}, {"id": "2", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/events"}, {"id": "3", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/drive/root/children"} ] } ``` ## OAuth 2.0 with Refresh Tokens ### Authorization Flow ``` 1. GET /authorize?client_id=...&scope=...&redirect_uri=... 2. User consents 3. POST /token (authorization_code grant) 4. Store refresh_token securely 5. POST /token (refresh_token grant) when access_token expires ``` ### Token Management ```yaml auth: type: "oauth2" client_id: "${AZURE_CLIENT_ID}" client_secret: "${AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET}" tenant_id: "${AZURE_TENANT_ID}" redirect_uri: "http://localhost:8080/callback" token_storage: type: "encrypted_file" path: "/secure/tokens.enc" auto_refresh: true refresh_buffer_seconds: 300 ``` ### Scopes by Sub-connector | Sub-connector | Required Scopes | |---------------|-----------------| | Outlook Mail | Mail.Read, Mail.ReadWrite, Mail.Send | | Outlook Calendar | Calendars.Read, Calendars.ReadWrite | | OneDrive | Files.Read, Files.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite.All | | Excel | Files.ReadWrite | | Word | Files.ReadWrite | | OneNote | Notes.Read, Notes.ReadWrite | | SharePoint | Sites.Read.All, Sites.ReadWrite.All | | Planner | Tasks.Read, Tasks.ReadWrite, Group.ReadWrite.All | ## Webhook Subscriptions (Real-time Events) ### Subscription Management ```python # Create subscription POST /subscriptions { "changeType": "created,updated,deleted", "notificationUrl": "https://webhook.example.com/m365", "resource": "/me/messages", "expirationDateTime": "2024-12-31T00:00:00Z", "clientState": "secret-validation-token" } ``` ### Supported Resources - `/me/messages`: Email changes - `/me/events`: Calendar changes - `/me/contacts`: Contact changes - `/drives/{id}/root`: OneDrive changes - `/sites/{id}/lists/{id}/items`: SharePoint changes - `/groups/{id}/planner/tasks`: Planner changes ### Webhook Handler ```python async def handle_webhook(request): # Validation challenge if 'validationToken' in request.query: return PlainTextResponse(request.query['validationToken']) # Process notifications for notification in request.json()['value']: resource = notification['resource'] change_type = notification['changeType'] # Process change... ``` ### Subscription Renewal - Max lifetime: 3 days (most resources) - Auto-renewal before expiration - Webhook validation handling ## Configuration Schema ```yaml fcp.microsoft365: tenant_id: "${AZURE_TENANT_ID}" client_id: "${AZURE_CLIENT_ID}" client_secret: "${AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET}" auth: type: "oauth2" redirect_uri: "http://localhost:8080/callback" token_cache: "/secure/token_cache.json" sub_connectors: outlook: enabled: true mail_folders: ["Inbox", "Sent Items"] calendar_sync: true onedrive: enabled: true root_path: "/Documents" excel: enabled: true word: enabled: true onenote: enabled: true sharepoint: enabled: true sites: ["https://tenant.sharepoint.com/sites/team"] planner: enabled: true webhooks: enabled: true endpoint_url: "https://webhook.example.com/m365" resources: - "/me/messages" - "/me/events" rate_limits: requests_per_second: 10 concurrent_connections: 5 ``` ## MCP Tool Definitions ### Outlook Tools - `outlook_list_messages`: List emails - `outlook_send_message`: Send email - `outlook_create_event`: Create calendar event - `outlook_get_availability`: Check free/busy ### OneDrive Tools - `onedrive_list_files`: List directory contents - `onedrive_upload_file`: Upload file - `onedrive_download_file`: Download file - `onedrive_share_file`: Create sharing link ### Excel Tools - `excel_read_range`: Read cell range - `excel_write_range`: Write to cells - `excel_create_table`: Create data table - `excel_run_function`: Execute formula ### SharePoint Tools - `sharepoint_list_sites`: List sites - `sharepoint_get_list_items`: Read list data - `sharepoint_create_list_item`: Add list item ### Planner Tools - `planner_list_tasks`: List user tasks - `planner_create_task`: Create new task - `planner_update_task`: Update task status ### Webhook Tools - `m365_create_subscription`: Create webhook - `m365_list_subscriptions`: List active webhooks - `m365_delete_subscription`: Remove webhook ## Enterprise Features ### Multi-tenant Support - Per-tenant configuration - Tenant isolation - Cross-tenant operations (with consent) ### Compliance & Governance - Audit logging - Data residency awareness - eDiscovery integration ### Delegated vs Application Permissions - User context operations - Background service operations - Admin consent flows ## Error Handling - Token refresh failures - Rate limit (429) handling - Throttling with Retry-After - Permission denied errors - Resource not found ## Implementation Phases ### Phase 1: Core Auth (Week 1-2) - [ ] OAuth 2.0 flow implementation - [ ] Token storage and refresh - [ ] Multi-tenant support - [ ] Permission management ### Phase 2: Outlook (Week 3-4) - [ ] Mail operations - [ ] Calendar operations - [ ] Contact operations ### Phase 3: OneDrive & Files (Week 5-6) - [ ] File operations - [ ] Excel integration - [ ] Word integration ### Phase 4: Collaboration (Week 7-8) - [ ] SharePoint integration - [ ] OneNote integration - [ ] Planner integration ### Phase 5: Real-time (Week 9-10) - [ ] Webhook subscriptions - [ ] Change notifications - [ ] Delta sync ### Phase 6: Enterprise (Week 11-12) - [ ] Compliance features - [ ] Monitoring - [ ] Documentation ## Dependencies - msal (Microsoft Authentication Library) - httpx (async HTTP client) - cryptography (token encryption) - pydantic (data validation) ## Success Metrics - OAuth flow success rate > 99% - API call latency < 500ms - Webhook delivery success > 99.9% - Support all listed sub-connectors - Enterprise security compliance","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:12:32.282999005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T07:27:59.269142268Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T07:27:59.269142268Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-m6u7","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","title":"fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research","description":"# fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research (FCP2) ## Goal Provide a research-focused macro surface that helps agents extract **authentic user opinions** from Reddit in a structured, bounded way. This is not “LLM analysis inside the connector”; it is data acquisition + packaging so agents can do downstream reasoning safely. ## Scope ### Research macros (compositions) - `reddit.research.search_threads`: - input: query + subreddit scope + time window - output: ranked thread summaries (ids, titles, scores, timestamps) - `reddit.research.collect_evidence`: - input: thread ids - output: bounded comment excerpts with provenance pointers (post/comment ids) ### Safety/bounds - Explicit caps: - max threads - max comments per thread - max total bytes returned - NSFW/quarantine policy must be explicit. ## Capability model (illustrative) - `reddit.read` - `reddit.search` - `reddit.research` (macro operations) ## FCP2 requirements - Single-zone binding. - NetworkConstraints pinned. - All content is tainted. - Structured logs include only ids + counts. ## Deliverables - Canonical output types: - `ResearchThreadSummary` - `ResearchEvidenceBundle` - Operation schemas: - `reddit.research.search_threads` - `reddit.research.collect_evidence` ## Test strategy (MUST) - Mock-only tests for: - ranking determinism - bounds enforcement - NSFW/quarantine gating ## Acceptance criteria - Research macros are deterministic, bounded, and mechanically authorized. ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:23.349836558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:12:58.677484Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T20:12:58.677469Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-xz6e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-xz6e.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1378,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented research.rs: ResearchQuery builder (subreddits, time window, score/comment thresholds), EvidenceRequest with bounded collection, ProvenancePointer for source attribution, Unicode-safe excerpt truncation. 142 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:12:54Z"}]} +{"id":"flywheel_connectors-d4cij","title":"[HIGH] fcp-host: verify_live_request policy gate uses permissive blank zone policy — admin deny rules never applied","description":"The policy-simulation gate in the host gateway is structurally present but toothless: every request runs through a ZonePolicyObject that is built fresh, in-process, with every pattern list empty and every transport allowed. Call chain: POST /invoke -> invoke_handler (bin/fcp-host.rs:3856) -> evaluate_live_preflight (bin/fcp-host.rs:1452) -> verify_live_request (bin/fcp-host.rs:1290) -> simulate_policy_decision (bin/fcp-host.rs:1411) zone_policy: host_runtime_policy(request.zone_id.clone()) host_runtime_policy (bin/fcp-host.rs:1016-1049) constructs: principal_allow: Vec::new() principal_deny: Vec::new() connector_allow: Vec::new() connector_deny: Vec::new() capability_allow: Vec::new() capability_deny: Vec::new() capability_ceiling: Vec::new() transport_policy: { allow_lan: true, allow_derp: true, allow_funnel: true } requires_posture: None usage_budget: None With those lists all empty, check_pattern_lists in fcp-core/src/policy.rs returns None every time (every `if !list.is_empty() && !matches_any(...)` short-circuits on `is_empty`). The only denial paths that remain under this blank policy are: - RevocationStaleFrontier / CheckpointStaleFrontier (freshness flags) - Taint-flag checks on risky+ safety tiers - apply_flow_approvals / execution_approval_required token mismatches Audit of the fcp-host tree finds NO zone-policy store anywhere: ZonePolicyObject is constructed only at bin/fcp-host.rs:1016-1049, and there is no admin-plane persistence or endpoint that accepts zone policy rules (grep over crates/fcp-host confirms). So even if an operator configured principal_deny/capability_deny/connector_deny pattern lists in some out-of-band config, the fcp-host gateway has nowhere to load them from — the gate looks like enforcement but is structurally a no-op for rule-based denials. This is the classic default-allow regression shape: a gate was added, the shape is correct, but the configuration pipeline to feed it is missing. Fix shape: 1. Add a ZonePolicyStore to AppState: Arc>> Persisted alongside connector inventory in admin_state (similar to connector_configs / pinned_versions / receipts). 2. New admin endpoints: GET/PUT /admin/zones/:zone_id/policy that parse+validate a ZonePolicyObject and persist it, with dry-run preview semantics matching connector inventory mutations. 3. verify_live_request looks up the policy for request.zone_id; falls back to host_runtime_policy only when the zone has no configured policy AND emits a WARN log so operators notice. 4. Integration test: configure a zone policy with `principal_deny = ["attacker:*"]`; verify a live invoke from `attacker:foo` is rejected at the gateway even with a valid capability token. Severity: HIGH. The gateway advertises policy-simulation denial (PreflightFailed("policy denied live request: {reason_code}") at bin/fcp-host.rs:1416) but rule-based denial cannot fire.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:55:30.661629Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:55:30.661629Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d4j2","title":"[IDEA-07.3] Formatting fallback tests + example wiring","description":"## Goal Provide tests and example wiring in a connector send_message path. ## Details - Add integration test with a fake API returning parse errors. - Example wiring notes in connector template or docs. ## Acceptance Criteria - Example shows how to hook formatter and error classifier together. - Tests confirm fallback behavior without leaking content in logs.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical] - Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases. - Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe. [ExtremeOptimizationPractical] - Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds. - Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost. [AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:35:24.864612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T15:14:48.424813164Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T15:14:48.424741721Z","close_reason":"Wired formatter/classifier in Telegram send_message; added parse-error fallback test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d4j2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-30bf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d5h","title":"fcp.datadog: Dashboard access and widget rendering","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:16:37.975697485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T17:10:13.135733516Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T17:10:13.135733516Z","close_reason":"CONSOLIDATED: All Datadog sub-features merged into comprehensive parent connector bead flywheel_connectors-cu3 (fcp.datadog: Datadog Observability Platform Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","title":"[tooling] MCP Agent Mail storage.sqlite3 corruption blocks registration and inbox access","description":"Agent Mail use in /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors is currently blocked before registration or inbox access. MCP tool calls such as macro_start_session, list_agents, ensure_project, and resource reads return DATABASE_ERROR with detail: refusing automatic sqlite recovery for /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/storage.sqlite3 while live WAL/SHM sidecars are present; stop the server and run explicit repair. Direct sqlite integrity_check confirms main DB corruption (freelist corruption, duplicate page references, out-of-order rowid, and many never-used pages). Current on-disk state includes storage.sqlite3, storage.sqlite3-wal, storage.sqlite3-shm, and storage.sqlite3.bak. This prevents the user-requested Agent Mail registration, inbox processing, and introductions to other agents. Acceptance: 1) Agent Mail can register/list agents/fetch inbox again in this repo, 2) repair is done with an explicit, auditable procedure that does not silently destroy state, 3) coordination notes capture that repair may require stopping the live MCP server and handling WAL/SHM explicitly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:55:37.245489Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-19T01:18:37.872420Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T01:18:37.871883Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-mail","coordination","sqlite","tooling"],"comments":[{"id":2046,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Non-destructive diagnosis on 2026-03-27: MCP Agent Mail health is green enough to answer health checks, but project-scoped operations fail with DATABASE_ERROR because /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/storage.sqlite3 is corrupt and live sidecars are present. Evidence collected: read_mcp_resource on resource://projects returns error_detail saying automatic sqlite recovery is refused while WAL/SHM are live; sqlite3 PRAGMA integrity_check reports freelist corruption, duplicate page references, and rowid/order damage; ls shows storage.sqlite3 plus storage.sqlite3-wal and storage.sqlite3-shm; lsof shows Agent Mail listener processes on port 8765 including /Users/jemanuel/.local/bin/am serve-http --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765 --no-tui. sqlite3 .recover emits a plausible SQL dump, so an explicit stop-and-recover procedure looks possible, but I am not running it without user approval because it would rewrite coordination state.","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:56:13Z"},{"id":2119,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyForest attempted to switch coordination back to Agent Mail after partial recovery. Current state is inconsistent: `fetch_inbox` and `list_agents` now succeed, but `send_message` fails with `no such table: message_recipients` after quarantine/recovery, and `file_reservation_paths` still returns a generic database error. This means mailbox reads work but delivery/reservations remain broken, so true coordination is still blocked. I am recording the failure here because the user explicitly asked for Agent Mail introductions and ongoing coordination, and the tool currently cannot complete that flow.","created_at":"2026-03-29T23:27:37Z"},{"id":2121,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyForest follow-up after SDK verification: the repo-local Agent Mail store remains unstable rather than merely partially repaired. During this pass it briefly allowed `fetch_inbox` and `list_agents`, but later the same calls returned generic database errors again, while `send_message` was already failing with `no such table: message_recipients` and file reservations were failing generically. Net: Agent Mail cannot currently be relied on for introductions, inbox polling, or reservations in this repo, even when one or two reads appear to work transiently.","created_at":"2026-03-29T23:45:47Z"},{"id":2131,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"SilverQuarry","text":"2026-03-30 fresh session symptom report: Agent Mail is still too broken to satisfy the user’s explicit coordination request. returns green, and for /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors succeeds, but , , , and all fail with the generic MCP response In this session I was therefore unable to register, list active agents, fetch inbox state, introduce myself, or reserve files through Agent Mail. This is consistent with the bead’s existing corruption history, but the current symptom surface is effectively total write-path failure rather than a stable partial recovery.","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:12:10Z"},{"id":2132,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"SilverQuarry","text":"2026-03-30 corrected symptom note. In this session Agent Mail health_check returned green and ensure_project for /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors succeeded, but macro_start_session, register_agent, list_agents, and create_agent_identity all failed with the generic MCP response A database error occurred. This may be a transient issue - try again. I therefore could not register, discover active agents, fetch inbox state, introduce myself, or reserve files through Agent Mail. Current behavior is effectively total write-path failure for the coordination flow the user requested.","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:13:03Z"},{"id":2142,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"BronzeCondor","text":"2026-03-31 BronzeCondor symptom update while following the user’s explicit coordination request. Blocked by provisioning bead.","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:22:01Z"}]} +{"id":"flywheel_connectors-zfogg","title":"[LOW] fcp-host policy engine rule-order + cache: no determinism or staleness bug found, documented for audit trail","description":"Positive-finding note from the fcp-host policy evaluation engine audit. Filed so future reviewers do not re-audit these specific angles. Rule order determinism: - check_pattern_lists in fcp-core/src/policy.rs:2717-2748 applies denials first (principal_deny, connector_deny, capability_deny) then allow-not-matched checks (principal_allow, connector_allow, capability_allow). Order is deterministic and fixed in source. - matches_any uses `patterns.iter().any(...)` which short-circuits on first match. .any() is deterministic over a slice. Order of patterns within a single list does not matter for match correctness (any vs all), only for performance of short-circuit. - PolicyEngine::evaluate_invoke (fcp-core/src/policy.rs:2482) applies its gates in a fixed sequence: freshness -> transport -> pattern lists -> posture -> capability_ceiling -> sanitizer receipts -> taint flags -> flow approvals -> execution approval -> provenance-can-drive. No data-driven reordering. - No iteration over HashMap that would surface nondeterministic order in a denial path. Policy-cache staleness: - The actual ZonePolicyObject is rebuilt fresh per request from host_runtime_policy (bin/fcp-host.rs:1016). No cache to go stale — though the policy is blank, see br-d4cij. - BudgetPolicyEngine reads policies and tracker live under RwLock/Mutex on every evaluate_preflight. No cache. - DiscoveryCache (discovery.rs:1301) caches ConnectorSummary lists for 30s keyed by registry_version. Invalidated by prepare_configs bumping version. Affects connector visibility, not policy decisions. Introspection (used by verify_live_request to find tool.capability) calls registry.get_introspection LIVE per request, only the summary-lookup half hits cache. Conclusion: no rule-order determinism bug and no policy-cache staleness bug at the fcp-host layer. Files referenced: crates/fcp-core/src/policy.rs:2482-2578 (evaluate_invoke), :2717-2752 (check_pattern_lists / matches_any). crates/fcp-host/src/budget.rs:364 (BudgetPolicyEngine::evaluate_preflight). crates/fcp-host/src/discovery.rs:1301 (DiscoveryCache), :1128 (introspect).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:55:50.787550Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:55:58.964143Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:55:58.963684Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2794,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-zfogg","author":"jemanuel","text":"Filed as a null-finding for audit trail. No action needed — documents that rule order is deterministic and no policy cache exists to go stale.","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:55:58Z"}]} {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zmwvq","title":"TEST: fcp-google-discovery test expansion (provisioning, auth, lib)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:09:14.840949Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T06:28:57.041491Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T06:28:57.041470Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":929,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-zmwvq","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"TEST: fcp-google-discovery test expansion complete. All 442 tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:28:14Z"}]} {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zt2h9","title":"fcp-streaming: make heartbeat, zombie, reconnect, and buffer constants env-overridable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:36.713051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:57.877386Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:57.877214Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","hardening","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-zt2h9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9fxph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:50.680420Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1952,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-zt2h9","author":"jemanuel","text":"AFFECTED FILES AND VALUES: - src/lib.rs:48: DEFAULT_RECONNECT_DELAY = Duration::from_secs(1) - src/lib.rs:51: MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY = Duration::from_secs(60) - src/lib.rs:54: DEFAULT_BUFFER_SIZE = 8192 - src/health.rs:65: heartbeat_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30) - src/health.rs:66: zombie_timeout: Duration::from_secs(120) - src/health.rs:67: max_reconnect_attempts: 10 - src/websocket.rs:404-411: connect_timeout 30s, ping_interval 30s, pong_timeout 10s, max_message_size 64MB, reconnect_delay 1s, max_reconnect_attempts 10 - src/sse.rs:262-266: max_buffer_size 1MB, max_reconnect_attempts 10, reconnect_delay 1s All of these are in Default::default() impls or module-level consts. They have builder/config struct overrides but no env-var path. Steps: 1. For each StreamHealthConfig, WsConfig, SseConfig default: check env var before using compiled default 2. Env var naming convention: FCP_STREAMING_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_SECS, FCP_STREAMING_ZOMBIE_TIMEOUT_SECS, FCP_STREAMING_MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS, FCP_STREAMING_BUFFER_SIZE, etc. 3. Add parse-with-fallback helper: fn env_or(var: &str, default: T) -> T 4. Document env vars in module-level rustdoc 5. Keep all existing builder methods working (explicit config > env var > compiled default) 6. Add tests verifying env var override path","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:50Z"}]} {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zx0","title":"fcp-ratelimit: Shared Rate Limiting Library","description":"# fcp-ratelimit: Shared Rate Limiting Library ## Overview Production-grade rate limiting library supporting multiple algorithms, provider-specific header parsing, and intelligent backoff strategies. ## Algorithms ### Token Bucket - Configurable bucket size and refill rate - Burst handling - Per-endpoint buckets ### Sliding Window - Fixed window with sliding calculation - Accurate request counting - Memory-efficient implementation ### Leaky Bucket - Smooth request pacing - Queue management - Overflow handling ## Provider Header Parsing - X-RateLimit-Limit/Remaining/Reset (standard) - Retry-After header - X-Rate-Limit-* variations - Provider-specific formats (GitHub, Twitter, Stripe, OpenAI) ## Backoff Strategies - Exponential backoff with configurable base/max - Decorrelated jitter - Truncated exponential - Custom strategy support ## Features - Thread-safe implementations - Async-first design - Request queueing with priorities - Quota tracking (daily/hourly/monthly) - Multi-tenant support ## Integration - Middleware for HTTP clients - Decorator for FCP operations - Metrics emission - Logging integration ## Dependencies - tokio (async), parking_lot (sync primitives) - fcp-core, fcp-telemetry","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:36:25.276288662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T18:04:26.849292962Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T18:04:26.849292962Z","close_reason":"Implemented fcp-ratelimit crate with token bucket, sliding/fixed window, leaky bucket algorithms, provider header parsing, and backoff strategies. All 20 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
